physiologicalParams.name: Resting heart rate
physiologicalParams.driver: Regular aerobic sessions increase stroke volume

physiologicalParams.name: Blood pressure
physiologicalParams.driver: Potassium-rich foods, moderate sodium, and consistent cardio

physiologicalParams.name: Fasting glucose
physiologicalParams.driver: High-fiber carbs and muscle taking up glucose after training

physiologicalParams.name: LDL / HDL cholesterol
physiologicalParams.expectedChange: LDL down ~5–10%, HDL up modestly over 3 months
physiologicalParams.driver: Fish, olive oil, nuts, and soluble fiber replacing saturated fat

physiologicalParams.name: VO2max
physiologicalParams.driver: Weekly running, cycling, and basketball push aerobic ceiling

physiologicalParams.name: Body-fat percentage
physiologicalParams.expectedChange: Gradual 1–2 point drop while weight stays stable
physiologicalParams.driver: High protein plus strength work shifts composition toward muscle

physiologicalParams.name: Muscle mass
physiologicalParams.driver: Three strength sessions weekly with ~1.6 g/kg protein

physiologicalParams.name: Sleep quality
physiologicalParams.expectedChange: Faster sleep onset and deeper sleep
physiologicalParams.driver: Daily exercise and a consistent eating rhythm

meals.items: Greek yogurt bowl with honey, walnut granola, and sliced banana
Chicken shawarma wrap with hummus, tabbouleh, and pickled turnips
Apple with peanut butter; glass of low-fat milk
Grilled salmon with lemon-herb couscous and olive-oil roasted zucchini and peppers

exercise.activity: Upper-body strength session with 10-min rowing warm-up

week.highlight: A protein-forward Mediterranean day fuels the week's first lifting session and keeps omega-3s high.

meals.items: Three-egg vegetable omelette, two slices whole-grain toast, and fresh orange juice
Chicken teriyaki rice bowl with edamame and pickled cucumber
Trail mix (almonds, cashews, raisins) and a banana; green tea
Thai basil beef stir-fry with jasmine rice and steamed broccoli

exercise.activity: 5K run at a steady, challenging pace

week.highlight: Higher-carb Asian-inspired meals top up glycogen around a short, intense run.

meals.items: Scrambled-egg and black bean breakfast burrito with salsa; banana
Chicken burrito bowl with brown rice, pinto beans, corn salsa, and guacamole
Cottage cheese with pineapple and whole-grain crackers
Baked cod tacos with cabbage slaw and avocado crema; cilantro-lime rice

exercise.activity: Lower-body strength session plus core work

week.highlight: Bean-rich Latin dishes push fiber near 40 g while supporting a heavy leg day.

meals.items: Oatmeal with blueberries, chia seeds, and walnuts; low-fat milk
Turkey and avocado sandwich on whole-grain bread; side salad, hummus with crackers, and a pear
Greek yogurt with strawberries, honey, granola, and almonds
Turkey chili with kidney beans over brown rice; cornbread on the side

week.highlight: Classic American comfort food, rebuilt around whole grains and legumes for steady energy on the bike.

meals.items: Masala scrambled eggs with whole-wheat paratha; small mango lassi
Chicken tikka with basmati rice, cucumber raita, and a cup of dal
Roasted chickpeas, a banana, and fresh mango
Vegetable and paneer curry with brown rice and chapati

week.highlight: An Indian-inspired day where dal, chickpeas, and paneer show plant-and-dairy protein can carry a training day.

meals.items: Whole-grain pancakes with mixed berries and maple syrup; scrambled eggs
Tuna pasta salad with olives, cherry tomatoes, and arugula
Smoothie of banana, oats, peanut butter, and milk
Baked chicken parmigiana with spaghetti and garlic green beans

week.highlight: The week's biggest eating and activity day: extra carbs fuel 75 minutes of basketball.

meals.items: Avocado toast with two poached eggs and tomato; glass of milk and an orange
Miso-glazed tofu grain bowl with quinoa, spinach, and sesame
Hummus with carrot sticks and whole-wheat pita; grapes
Roast chicken with sweet potato mash, green salad, and a wholegrain roll

exercise.activity: Easy 30-minute walk plus 15 minutes of stretching

week.highlight: A deliberate recovery day: gentle movement and slightly lighter intake help muscles adapt.

micronutrients.name: Calcium
micronutrients.keySources: Milk, Greek yogurt, cottage cheese, paneer

micronutrients.name: Iron
micronutrients.keySources: Beef stir-fry, turkey chili, beans, fortified oats

micronutrients.name: Potassium
micronutrients.keySources: Bananas, sweet potato, beans, yogurt, spinach

micronutrients.name: Magnesium
micronutrients.keySources: Nuts, whole grains, beans, leafy greens

micronutrients.name: Zinc
micronutrients.keySources: Beef, chicken, chickpeas, pumpkin-seed trail mix

nutrition: Roughly 1.6 g of protein per kg body weight, spread over four meals, keeps muscle protein synthesis elevated through the day.
35–40 g of daily fiber from beans, oats, and whole grains steadies blood sugar and feeds a healthier gut microbiome.
Fish twice a week plus olive oil and nuts shifts blood-lipid balance toward lower LDL over a few months.
Carbs concentrated around training days refill glycogen so runs and games feel strong instead of flat.
Potassium-rich produce with sodium kept under 2300 mg supports healthy blood pressure now and later in life.
exercise: Three strength sessions a week trigger measurable muscle growth and a stronger, denser skeleton by the late 20s peak-bone window.
One vigorous run plus a basketball game raise VO2max, one of the best long-term predictors of health.
Mixing lifting with cardio improves insulin sensitivity for 24–48 hours after each session.
About 2,470 kcal of weekly activity makes maintaining a healthy weight nearly automatic at this intake.
A true recovery day with walking and stretching lets tissues adapt and cuts overuse-injury risk.

notes: The 18–35 window is when strength, bone density, and aerobic capacity peak — habits built now set the baseline for every later decade. This sample week shows one balanced way to hit those targets; individual needs vary with size and activity.
